title: European Union Outlines Plan to Reduce Dependence on American Tech
description: The 27-nation European Union outlined how it hopes to expand the region’s data centers, semiconductors and cloud computing capabilities.
author: Adam Satariano

title: Trump Signs Executive Order Seeking Oversight of A.I. Models
description: The order, which signaled a shift from the hands-off approach the White House had previously taken toward A.I., followed debates over how to gain control of A.I. models without disrupting innovation.
author: Sheera Frenkel and Tripp Mickle

title: Scientists Find Way to Supercharge Dangerous Computer ‘Worms’ With A.I.
description: Researchers at the University of Toronto showed how hackers could use artificial intelligence to create a program that could target any known flaw in the world’s computers.
author: Cade Metz

title: Anthropic Files to Go Public, Setting Stage for Huge I.P.O.
description: The artificial intelligence company, which is racing OpenAI to the stock market, has seen explosive growth over the last year thanks largely to technology that can automatically write computer code.
author: Mike Isaac

title: Meta Expands Safety Features for Teenagers
description: The changes, after Meta’s legal losses in two child safety cases, are aimed at limiting harmful content shown to teenagers on Instagram, Facebook and Messenger.
author: Eli Tan

title: How Box Created 13 New Types of Jobs Because of A.I.
description: Box, a Silicon Valley software maker, expects to have more employees, not fewer, as it hires A.I. architects, A.I. solutions managers and other new A.I.-related positions.
author: Kalley Huang

title: Florida Sues OpenAI Over Chatbot Safety Concerns
description: The state became the first to sue the ChatGPT maker over claims that its technology posed a risk to children and that the company had failed to warn the public of dangers.
author: David McCabe

title: Is A.I. Replacing Tech Workers or Providing an Excuse for Job Cuts?
description: Tech industry layoffs are accelerating, and executives have been quick to say it’s because their companies are doing more with artificial intelligence, even when there may be more to it.
author: Kalley Huang

title: China Aims A.I. at Predicting Who Could Pose a Political Risk
description: New research examines how a Chinese company struggled to develop its predictive surveillance technology while U.S. restrictions were in place.
author: Julian E. Barnes

title: Chinese Military Sought Nvidia Chips for Years, Report Says
description: An analysis of six years of procurement records suggests that the People’s Liberation Army has openly tried to acquire restricted U.S. technology.
author: Ana Swanson and Tripp Mickle

title: Nvidia Has a Plan to Put Its Chips in Personal Computers
description: The world’s most valuable company is chasing Intel and Apple as it tries to bring A.I. agents to laptops and desktops.
author: Tripp Mickle

title: Spencer Pratt Wants to Be Mayor. His Fans Want Him to Be Batman.
description: What an A.I.-generated video of the former reality TV star teaches us about the future of political ads.
author: Willy Staley

title: Teachers Union Urges Schools to Curb A.I. Chatbots and Screen Time
description: The American Federation of Teachers recommended “no screens” at all for those in second grade or younger, and no A.I. chatbots for students in elementary school.
author: Natasha Singer
